MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jun-22-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant Rickshaw Stop's motion for summary judgment is denied. Rickshaw Stop has not satisfied its initial summary judgment burden showing that the doctrine of primary assumption of risk bars plaintiff Kevin Paterson's recovery for the injuries he allegedly suffered. The facts relied on by Rickshaw Stop do not establish that Mr. Paterson was voluntarily participating in any activity other than attending a concert at the time he was injured. Attending a concert is not an activity that implicates the doctrine of primary assumption of risk. Even if the facts submitted by Rickshaw Stop established that Mr. Paterson was voluntarily participating in moshpit dancing at the time he was injured, which they do not, under the current state of California law moshpit dancing and all other forms of recreational dancing are not activities that implicate the doctrine of primary assumption of risk. (Bush v. Parents Without Partners (1993) 17 Cal. App. 4th 322). Rickshaw Stop's efforts to distinguish Bush are unavailing. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR # 11185 =302/hk
